description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, slightly mottled stone surface. The block has sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness, suggesting it was precisely cut or manufactured. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with the grooved pattern only present on the top face. The coloration is primarily a mix of gray and muted brown tones, giving it a weathered, utilitarian appearance. This type of block is commonly used as a drainage or ventilation cover in urban environments, such as sidewalks or roadways.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, featuring a striped gray and brown appearance. The block is solid and robust, typical of urban drainage or ventilation covers.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the grooves running vertically from this perspective.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same grooved pattern across the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage system
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: Slight surface discoloration and mottling suggest exposure to weather and foot traffic, but no significant erosion or chipping is visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or concrete surfaces
